sources.title: State of Nevada Board of Wildlife Commissioners — Fishing Seasons CR 25-16 (Jan 1, 2026 – Dec 31, 2027)
sources.quotedText: Washoe County Community Fishing Ponds: Battle Born Pond, Davis Creek Pond, Idlewild Ponds, Marilyn's Pond, Paradise Ponds, Rancho San Rafael Pond, Sparks Marina Pond, Verdi Pond, Virginia Lake and Wilson Common. Open year around. Daily: 3 game fish. Possession: 6 game fish. Hours parks are open to public use.

sources.title: Marilyn's Pond — Nevada Department of Wildlife
sources.quotedText: This 1-acre pond is approximately 10 feet deep and is a great pond to introduce youngsters to fishing. The shoreline provides easy access and kids are almost certain to catch rainbow trout or even brook trout from 8 to 10 inches. The park has restrooms and picnic facilities, and the pond has an accessible fishing pier. Stocking Updates (selected): 532 Rainbow Trout, 9.8 in, 06-08-2026; 499 Rainbow Trout, 10.3 in, 05-11-2026; 600 Rainbow Trout, 10.1 in, 04-27-2026; 318 Rainbow Trout, 9.9 in, 04-13-2026.

sources.title: Apply & Buy — Nevada Fishing Licenses — NDOW
sources.quotedText: Anyone 12 years of age or older is required to have a fishing license in their possession while in the field.

sources.title: Fishbrain — Marilyn's Pond fishing water coordinates (used because the USGS GNIS ArcGIS gazetteer (nationalmap.gov geonames MapServer/7, and MapServer/6) has no queryable record for "Marilyn's Pond" or any similar name in Washoe County, NV — this small community fishing pond is not GNIS-indexed)
sources.quotedText: 39°21′10.7″N, 119°51′33.8″W = 39.3530°N, 119.8594°W, matching the file's recorded coordinate (-119.859, 39.353) to within ~15m.

regulations.citation: NBWC CR 25-16 — Washoe County Community Fishing Ponds
regulations.humanSummary: Marilyn's Pond is open to fishing year-round.

parameters.counting_rule: No species-specific sub-limit is stated for Marilyn's Pond in NDOW's current regulation table; the limit applies to the combined daily/possession total across all game fish species.

regulations.citation: NBWC CR 25-16 — Washoe County Community Fishing Ponds (incl. Marilyn's Pond)
regulations.humanSummary: Daily limit 3 game fish; possession limit 6 game fish (all species combined) at Marilyn's Pond.
regulations.verbatimText: Daily: 3 game fish. Possession: 6 game fish.

parameters.note: NDOW's commission-regulation table lists hours for Washoe County Community Fishing Ponds generically as 'hours parks are open to public use'; no fixed clock times or sunrise/sunset offsets are specified for Marilyn's Pond (within Galena Creek Regional Park).

regulations.citation: NBWC CR 25-16 — Washoe County Community Fishing Ponds
regulations.humanSummary: Fishing at Marilyn's Pond is allowed during the hours the park is open to the public.
regulations.verbatimText: Hours parks are open to public use

parameters.note: Standard Nevada resident/nonresident fishing license required for anglers 12 and older; no water-specific permit exists for Marilyn's Pond.

regulations.humanSummary: A Nevada fishing license is required for anglers age 12 and older at Marilyn's Pond; no license is required under age 12.
regulations.verbatimText: Anyone 12 years of age or older is required to have a fishing license in their possession while in the field.
